_note: FlowSchema. Seeded from docs/audits/2026-06-flowschema-property-liveness.md. Consumers: service-automation engine + node executors. runAs is marker-driven experimental (spec). status/active gate nothing (engine uses an in-memory flowEnabled map via toggleFlow).

status.note: LIVE via objectui metadata-admin authoring UI — the 2026-06 audit missed objectui; verified by reading the file.

active.note: LIVE via objectui metadata-admin authoring UI — the 2026-06 audit missed objectui; verified by reading the file.

runAs.evidence: packages/services/service-automation/src/builtin/crud-nodes.ts: CRUD nodes pass the run identity to ObjectQL per flow.runAs; the engine sets context.runAs at run setup; resolveRunDataContext maps system->isSystem / user->trigger user (#1888)
runAs.note: ENFORCED 2026-06-24 (#1888, ADR-0049): was marker-driven experimental; the engine now switches the data-layer execution identity and restores the caller context. Proof-backed by the dogfood gate.

template.authorHint: No reader in designer or engine — flagging a flow as a template/subflow does nothing. Invoke shared logic via a subflow node referencing the flow by name.

type.note: open string vs live executor registry (ADR-0018); FlowNodeAction enum is out of sync. ADR-0054 high-risk class (flow nodes): the proof authors a flow, triggers it over HTTP, and asserts the update_record node executed AND the input variable wired into its filter (only the targeted record changed) — guarding node execution + variable wiring, not just that the engine reads the node.

outputSchema.authorHint: Declared but never validated — node outputs are NOT checked against this schema at runtime. Do not rely on it for contract enforcement.

boundaryConfig.note: LIVE via objectui metadata-admin authoring UI — the 2026-06 audit missed objectui; verified by reading the file.

fallbackNodeId.evidence: engine uses per-node fault edges, not this
fallbackNodeId.authorHint: The engine routes failures via per-node fault edges, not errorHandling.fallbackNodeId — wire a fault edge from the failing node instead.
